title: iphiona phillipsiae
intro: Iphiona phillipsiae, also known as the African or American wormseed, is a flowering plant in the daisy family. Traditionally used as an anthelmintic, Iphiona phillipsiae has been employed to treat worm infestations, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve digestive issues, and act as an anti-parasitic agent. Iphiona phillipsiae is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the specific preparation and individual circumstances.
